name: China
welcome: Nǐ hǎo! Welcome to China — home of the giant panda, the Great Wall, and ancient dragon legends!
summary: China is the most populated country in the world and one of the oldest civilisations.
It invented paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass — four things that changed the world!
China is a land of towering mountains, flowing rivers, bamboo forests, and amazing pandas.

LOCATION.intro: China is a huge country in eastern Asia — let's find it!

items.title: China in Asia
narration: China is the third-largest country in the world by area.
It borders 14 different countries — more than almost any other nation!
The Yangtze and Yellow Rivers run through China — they have fed Chinese people for thousands of years.

LANDMARKS.intro: China has some of the most extraordinary landmarks in the world!

items.title: The Great Wall
narration: The Great Wall of China is the longest structure ever built by humans!
It stretches over 21,000 kilometres across mountains and deserts.
Hundreds of thousands of workers built it over many centuries to protect China.

items.title: The Forbidden City
narration: The Forbidden City in Beijing was the palace of Chinese emperors for 500 years!
It has 9,999 rooms — because only heaven is allowed to have 10,000 rooms.
Ordinary people were forbidden from entering — that is how it got its name!

items.title: Zhangjiajie Mountains
narration: The Zhangjiajie mountains look like something from a fantasy movie!
Giant pillars of rock shoot straight up from the forest floor.
These floating mountains inspired the floating islands in the film Avatar!

ANIMALS.intro: China is home to some truly magical animals!

items.title: Giant Panda
narration: The giant panda is China's most beloved animal and its national treasure!
They eat bamboo for up to 16 hours a day — they must really love it!
Giant pandas are very rare, and China works hard to protect them.

items.title: Red Panda
narration: The red panda looks like a cross between a fox, a raccoon, and a cat!
Its fluffy, rusty-red fur keeps it warm in mountain forests.
Red pandas use their long bushy tails as a cosy blanket while they sleep!

FOOD.intro: Chinese food is one of the most diverse and delicious cuisines in the world!

items.title: Dumplings
narration: Dumplings — called jiaozi — are little parcels of dough filled with meat and vegetables!
They are steamed, boiled, or fried until they are perfectly delicious.
Chinese families make dumplings together at Lunar New Year — it is a family tradition!

items.title: Noodles
narration: China invented noodles over 4,000 years ago!
There are hundreds of noodle dishes across China — thick, thin, flat, or round.
Long noodles are eaten at birthdays — they symbolise a long life!

LANGUAGE.intro: Chinese is written with characters that look like little pictures!

items.title: Nǐ hǎo — Hello
narration: Nǐ hǎo means hello in Mandarin Chinese!
Say it with me — nee HOW!
Chinese is written in thousands of characters — each one is a beautiful little drawing.

FESTIVALS.intro: Chinese festivals are full of colour, dragons, and fireworks!

items.title: Chinese New Year
narration: Chinese New Year is the biggest festival in China — and in much of Asia!
Families come together, fireworks fill the sky, and red envelopes of money are given to children.
A different animal represents each year — the rat, ox, tiger, rabbit, dragon, and more!

items.title: Lantern Festival
narration: At the end of Chinese New Year comes the magical Lantern Festival!
Thousands of glowing paper lanterns are released into the night sky.
It looks like a river of stars floating upward into the darkness.

items.title: Great Inventions
narration: China invented four things that changed the entire world: paper, printing, the compass, and gunpowder!
Before paper, people wrote on stone, clay, or bamboo — very heavy!
Without the compass, explorers could not have sailed the oceans and discovered new lands.

items.title: One Billion People
narration: China has over 1.4 billion people — that is one in every five humans on Earth!
Shanghai, China's biggest city, has more people than most entire countries.
China's bullet trains are the fastest in the world — they can reach 350 kilometres per hour!
